county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Williamstown. Formed 1820 from Pendleton. Northern Kentucky county. Grant County Historical Society. Williamstown city records. Dry Ridge community records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Pendleton County for pre-1820 records. Tobacco farming heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1820. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Grant County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1820.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mayfield. Records intact from 1823 formation. Far western Kentucky (Jackson Purchase). Graves County Historical Society. Mayfield is a regional center with city directories. Wooldridge Monuments in Mayfield Cemetery. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Hickman County for pre-1823 records. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1823 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1823. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Graves County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1823.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Leitchfield. Formed 1810 from Hardin and Ohio. Central-western Kentucky. Grayson County Historical Society. Jack Thomas House. Rough River Lake area. FamilySearch microfilm. Parent county for Edmonson. Check Hardin and Ohio counties for pre-1810 ancestors. Predominantly Unionist during Civil War.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1810 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1810. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Grayson County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1810.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Greensburg. One of Kentucky's first counties after statehood (1792 from Lincoln and Nelson). South-central Kentucky. Green County Historical Society. Green River heritage — the county and river are named for General Nathanael Greene. Parent county for Adair and Taylor. FamilySearch microfilm. Records well-preserved from 1793. Early settlers via Wilderness Road.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1792 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1792. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Green County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1792.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Greenup. Formed 1803 from Mason. Northeastern Kentucky on the Ohio River. Greenup County Historical Society. Ashland oil refinery records (now Marathon). Jesse Stuart's W-Hollow is in Greenup County. Iron furnace records (Amanda Furnace, others). Raceland-Worthington industrial records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1803 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1803. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Greenup County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1803.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hawesville. Formed 1829 from Breckinridge, Daviess, and Ohio. Ohio River county in western Kentucky. Hancock County Historical Society. Lewisport river town records. Small county — records well-preserved. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1829 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1829 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1829. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1829.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Elizabethtown. Records well-preserved from 1792 formation at statehood. Central Kentucky — Abraham Lincoln birthplace area (Hodgenville, now in Larue County which was formed from Hardin). Fort Knox military post (1918+) records. Hardin County Historical Society. Elizabethtown is a regional center with city directories. Helm Place historical records. Major parent county for several others. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1792 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1792. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Harlan. Formed 1819 from Knox. Southeastern Kentucky mountains. Coal mining is central to Harlan County history — 'Bloody Harlan' labor conflicts. Company town records, mine payrolls at Southeast Kentucky Community and Technical College. Portal No. 31 mining museum. Harlan County Historical Society. Benham and Lynch coal camp records. FamilySearch microfilm. Strong Appalachian heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1819. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Harlan County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1819.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

source_profiles.source_name: Harlan County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cynthiana. Formed 1793 from Bourbon and Scott. Inner Bluegrass region. Battle of Cynthiana (1862, 1864) Civil War records. Harrison County Historical Society. Cynthiana-Harrison County Museum. Thoroughbred horse farm records. Covered bridge heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Bourbon County for pre-1793 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1793 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1793. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Munfordville. Formed 1819 from Barren and Hardin. South-central Kentucky. Battle of Munfordville (1862) Civil War records. Hart County Historical Society. Green River area. L&N Railroad records document workers and communities. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Barren and Hardin counties for pre-1819 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1819. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
